text:
Custom orders of the Tesla Model S & X have come to an end. All that’s left are some in inventory.

We will have an official ceremony to mark the ending of an era. I love those cars. 

This was me at production launch 14 years ago: https://t.co/6kvCf9HTHc
summary: Custom orders of the Tesla Model S & X have come to an end. All that’s left are some in inventory. We will have an official ceremony to mark the ending of an era. I love those cars. This was me at production launch 14 years ago:
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced that Tesla has discontinued custom orders for Model S and X vehicles, with only existing inventory remaining available for purchase. This represents a significant product lifecycle decision for Tesla's original vehicle lines, signaling a strategic pivot likely toward next-generation models and manufacturing optimization. The announcement demonstrates how mature product lines are managed and transitioned within large-scale automotive manufacturing operations.
embed_html:
Custom orders of the Tesla Model S & X have come to an end. All that’s left are some in inventory.

We will have an official ceremony to mark the ending of an era. I love those cars.

This was me at production launch 14 years ago: pic.twitter.com/6kvCf9HTHc

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 1, 2026

text: FSD 14.3 is in Tesla employee beta now and will probably go to wide release end of week
summary: FSD 14.3 is in Tesla employee beta now and will probably go to wide release end of week
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced that Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) 14.3 software version is currently in employee beta testing with plans for wide public release by end of week. This represents a product development milestone for Tesla's autonomous driving technology, demonstrating the company's rapid iteration cycle and progression toward deploying advanced AI-driven features to its vehicle fleet.
embed_html:
FSD 14.3 is in Tesla employee beta now and will probably go to wide release end of week

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 1, 2026

text: Tesla Supercharger energy delivered is growing exponentially https://t.co/6hEQ4FKDyN
summary: Tesla Supercharger energy delivered is growing exponentially
ai_summary: Elon Musk reports that Tesla's Supercharger network is experiencing exponential growth in energy delivery, indicating expanding infrastructure adoption and usage. This demonstrates Tesla's progress in building out critical charging infrastructure—a key competitive advantage and business metric that supports EV adoption rates and reinforces Tesla's ecosystem moat as a vertically integrated automaker.
embed_html:
Tesla Supercharger energy delivered is growing exponentially https://t.co/6hEQ4FKDyN

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 1, 2026

text:

It will greatly improve your quality of life and may save your life. https://t.co/4PIrmkQSip
summary: Try out self-driving in a Tesla. It will greatly improve your quality of life and may save your life.

text:
Grok Imagine makes it easy and fast to create fun videos!

Young kids especially love doing image & video generation using voice mode (press the speak button). 

And you can turn on kid safe mode in settings: https://t.co/z5NPAAjjaA
summary: Grok Imagine makes it easy and fast to create fun videos! Young kids especially love doing image & video generation using voice mode (press the speak button). And you can turn on kid safe mode in settings:
ai_summary: Elon announces that Grok Imagine, xAI's video generation feature, now supports voice mode for faster content creation and includes a kid-safe mode option. The business insight is that xAI is expanding Grok's capabilities beyond text to multimodal content generation (image and video), targeting both general users and families as potential user segments, demonstrating product diversification and feature velocity in the competitive AI assistant market.

text: @JessePeltan There is more than enough surface area in Japan to power the entire country with solar without infringing on farming or destroying forests
summary: @JessePeltan There is more than enough surface area in Japan to power the entire country with solar without infringing on farming or destroying forests

parent.text: Japan needs HVDC lines and batteries like a fish needs water.
parent.name: Jesse Peltan
parent.embed_html:
Japan needs HVDC lines and batteries like a fish needs water. https://t.co/Fvbskm9fsS pic.twitter.com/J0WPzxNyft

— Jesse Peltan (@JessePeltan) April 1, 2026

ai_summary: Jesse Peltan argues Japan needs HVDC transmission lines and battery storage infrastructure to support renewable energy adoption, while Elon counters that Japan has sufficient surface area for solar deployment alone without agricultural or environmental tradeoffs. This reflects Tesla's energy storage business strategy and the broader debate over optimal infrastructure solutions for grid decarbonization—contrasting between transmission/storage infrastructure investments versus maximizing distributed renewable generation capacity.
embed_html:
There is more than enough surface area in Japan to power the entire country with solar without infringing on farming or destroying forests

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 1, 2026
